chapter_context.summary: Asa foolishly allies with Syria against Baasha; Hanuni the seer rebukes him and is imprisoned; Asa suffers from a foot disease and dies.

outline.text: Asa Bribes Ben-hadad to Break Treaty with Baasha of Israel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Asa Bribes Ben-hadad to Break Treaty with Baasha of Israel (16:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Hanani the Seer Rebukes Asa; Asa Angry and Imprisons Hanani
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hanani the Seer Rebukes Asa; Asa Angry and Imprisons Hanani (16:7-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Asa's Foot Disease, Death, and Funeral Spices
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Asa's Foot Disease, Death, and Funeral Spices (16:11-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: The Temple Centered Kingdom
key_themes.description: The presence of God's glory in the Temple is the absolute center of national blessing and life.

key_themes.title: Immediate Retribution and Reform
key_themes.description: Good kings who seek the LORD experience immediate victory and peace, while unfaithfulness leads to immediate warning and exile.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 16 prompt you to humble yourself, seek God's face, and turn away from compromise?
What practical reforms can you make in your daily walk to ensure your heart is dedicated to God?

cultural_historical.title: The Levitical Worship System
cultural_historical.text: Unlike the Book of Kings, Chronicles highlights the roles of singers, Levites, and liturgical musicians in the temple's daily administration.
